# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import inspect
import pandas.tests.indexes.test_numeric as mod

def test_test_equals_numeric_other_index_type_is_parametrized__a1():
    """
    The review comment asked to paramaterize the "other index type" equality checks.
    The addressed version introduces a dedicated parametrized test method:
    `test_equals_numeric_other_index_type`.

    This test asserts that the new parametrized test exists in the module and
    is marked with pytest.parametrize.
    """
    assert hasattr(mod.TestFloat64Index, 'test_equals_numeric_other_index_type'), "Expected TestFloat64Index.test_equals_numeric_other_index_type to exist; this indicates the 'other index type' equals checks were refactored into a parametrized test as requested in the review."

def test_test_equals_numeric_other_index_type_is_parametrized__a2():
    """
    The review comment asked to paramaterize the "other index type" equality checks.
    The addressed version introduces a dedicated parametrized test method:
    `test_equals_numeric_other_index_type`.

    This test asserts that the new parametrized test exists in the module and
    is marked with pytest.parametrize.
    """
    fn = mod.TestFloat64Index.test_equals_numeric_other_index_type
    marks = getattr(fn, 'pytestmark', [])
    has_parametrize = any((getattr(m, 'name', None) == 'parametrize' for m in marks))
    assert has_parametrize, f'Expected test_equals_numeric_other_index_type to be decorated with pytest.mark.parametrize, but it was not. Found source:\n\n{inspect.getsource(fn)}'
